assertions.title: Referral progresses through staged workflow with matching assessment

steps.action: Create referral 'Mason Reed' at /child-core-b-home/admissions/new and open the referral detail page.
steps.expected: Referral starts at Received and is traceable in the workflow timeline.
steps.observed: Mason Reed was created, listed on Admissions, and the timeline recorded None → Received with timestamp 02/04/2026 11:56:37.

steps.action: Complete the matching/impact assessment for Mason Reed.
steps.expected: Referral moves to Assessment Complete with risk rating and recommendation saved on the referral record.
steps.observed: Assessment saved with risk-to medium, risk-from medium, overall medium, recommendation accept_with_conditions, and the timeline recorded Received → Assessment Complete at 11:57:56.

steps.action: Record an acceptance decision, complete the admission checklist, and finish admission for Mason Reed.
steps.expected: Referral moves through Accepted to Admitted after checklist completion.
steps.observed: The decision was saved, all 10 checklist items could be completed, and the timeline recorded Assessment Complete → Accepted at 11:58:21 and Accepted → Admitted at 12:00:49.

steps.action: Create referral 'Lily Shaw', complete a high-risk assessment, and decline it with a reason.
steps.expected: Declined referrals are archived with the reason preserved.
steps.observed: The decline reason was saved and the timeline recorded Assessment Complete → Declined at 12:02:43, but Lily Shaw remained on the main Admissions list with no archive/filter separation.

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log shows POST /child-core-b-home/admissions/new -> 200 for both referrals; POST /child-core-b-home/admissions/55c7ce0b-93f1-4285-a7e6-b4d3c0323523 -> 200 for assessment, decision, checklist toggles, and admission; POST /child-core-b-home/admissions/acbfd9b1-8611-4f95-a2da-23c539912898 -> 200 for assessment and decline.

assertions.issues: The decline path does not archive declined referrals; Lily Shaw remained on the main admissions list instead of moving to an archive view.

assertions.title: Any staff member can record a safeguarding concern with verbatim capture and body map

steps.action: Invite/register the secondary carer account and open /child-core-b-home/safeguarding/concerns/new for Ava Turner.
steps.expected: A non-privileged staff member can access a concern form with verbatim capture and a body-map annotation tool.
steps.observed: The invited carer could reach the form, which included a 'Child's Own Words (Verbatim)' field, but the body-map surface was only a toggle exposing a plain 'Body Map Record ID' text input rather than an annotation tool.

steps.action: Enter valid concern details and submit the form multiple times (direct click and semantic button click).
steps.expected: The concern should be created immediately and become visible for DSL follow-up.
steps.observed: After filling a valid date/time, severity, description, verbatim account, presentation, category, location, witnesses, and immediate actions, clicking Submit Concern never navigated away or issued any POST request. The page stayed on the form with no console error.

steps.action: Attempt to continue to DSL review / immutability verification from the submitted concern.
steps.expected: The saved concern should exist and be immutable after submission.
steps.observed: No concern record was created, so DSL visibility and immutability enforcement could not be exercised from the real UI.

evidence.network: agent-browser request log showed repeated GET /child-core-b-home/safeguarding/concerns/new?... -> 200, but no POST request was emitted on any submit attempt. The shared dev-server log likewise contains no POST for the concern route.

assertions.issues: The concern form is not submittable from the real UI, and the 'body map' surface is only a record-ID field rather than an annotation tool.

assertions.title: DSL reviews concern and records decision with onward referral tracking

steps.expected: A DSL dashboard should list open concerns and allow decisions for internal monitoring, MASH, LADO, or police referral with referral tracking fields.
steps.observed: The route rendered only a placeholder panel stating that the DSL Review Dashboard will display open concerns once authentication is configured. No concern list, detail view, or decision form was present.

steps.action: Verify chronology linkage for concern and referral decisions.
steps.expected: The reviewed concern and onward referral should appear in the child's safeguarding chronology.
steps.observed: The chronology route is also a placeholder, so concern-to-referral linkage could not be verified through the UI.

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log shows GET /child-core-b-home/safeguarding/dsl-review -> 200 and GET /child-core-b-home/safeguarding/chronology/1098b5fe-83fa-4dac-a50d-f16f25bb37b9 -> 200, but both routes only render placeholder content.

assertions.issues: Blocked because the DSL review user surface is not implemented beyond a placeholder, and no submitted concern exists for review because VAL-CHILD-008 failed.

assertions.title: LADO referrals (allegations against staff) and Section 47 enquiries tracked separately

steps.action: Log in as the invited carer and navigate directly to /child-core-b-home/safeguarding/lado.
steps.expected: Standard staff should be denied access to restricted LADO records.
steps.observed: The carer account loaded the LADO Referrals page successfully and saw the restricted-access placeholder content instead of an access-denied state.

steps.action: Review the LADO and Section 47 routes for usable tracking workflows.
steps.expected: The UI should expose separate tracking for LADO allegations and Section 47 enquiries, including the required details and chronology linkage.
steps.observed: Both /safeguarding/lado and /safeguarding/section47 render placeholder panels only; there is no LADO referral form/list, no Section 47 tracking form/list, and no chronology integration surface.

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log shows GET /child-core-b-home/safeguarding/lado -> 200 for the staff account and GET /child-core-b-home/safeguarding/section47 -> 200 for the owner account, but both pages only expose placeholder content.

assertions.issues: Restricted-access enforcement is missing on the LADO route for standard staff, and neither the LADO nor Section 47 workflows are implemented beyond placeholder pages.

assertions.title: Risk assessment evaluates impact of new child on existing residents and vice versa

steps.action: Open Mason Reed's referral and inspect the Assessment tab before completion.
steps.expected: The assessment form should cover risk-to, risk-from, compatibility/capacity, and overall recommendation sections.
steps.observed: The form included risk-to and risk-from rating/narrative sections, occupancy and beds-available capacity fields, overall risk, recommendation, rationale, and optional conditions.

steps.expected: A risk rating and recommendation should be saved and linked to the referral record.
steps.observed: Submitting the assessment saved medium risk ratings and an accept_with_conditions recommendation, unlocked the Decision tab, and persisted a read-only 'Matching / impact risk assessment' panel on the referral.

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log shows POST /child-core-b-home/admissions/55c7ce0b-93f1-4285-a7e6-b4d3c0323523 -> 200 when the assessment was submitted.

assertions.title: Child's safeguarding chronology auto-generated from all concern, referral, and incident records

steps.expected: A read-only chronology should aggregate safeguarding events and expose Add Entry / Export PDF controls where applicable.
steps.observed: The route only displayed placeholder text saying chronology will display once authentication is configured. No entries, no manual-entry control, and no PDF export button were present.

steps.action: Attempt to validate auto-generated entries from concerns/referrals.
steps.expected: Concern and referral events should appear chronologically for the child.
steps.observed: Chronology population could not be tested because the chronology surface is not implemented and the concern form in VAL-CHILD-008 never created a record.

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log shows GET /child-core-b-home/safeguarding/chronology/1098b5fe-83fa-4dac-a50d-f16f25bb37b9 -> 200, but the page is placeholder-only.

assertions.issues: Blocked because the chronology page is not implemented beyond a placeholder and no concern record was created to feed it.

assertions.title: Safeguarding-to-action flow

steps.action: As the invited carer, attempt the first step of the flow by submitting a safeguarding concern for Ava Turner.
steps.expected: A submitted concern should become immediately visible to the DSL and preserve the verbatim recording.
steps.observed: The form captured the verbatim text field but no submit attempt issued a POST request, so no concern was created.

steps.action: As the owner, open the DSL dashboard and chronology routes to continue the flow.
steps.expected: The DSL should review the concern, track onward referrals, and maintain a full chronological history per child.
steps.observed: The DSL review page and chronology page both rendered placeholders only, so there was no real user surface for decisioning, referral tracking, or chronology review.

evidence.network: No POST was observed for /child-core-b-home/safeguarding/concerns/new on submit attempts; downstream routes GET /child-core-b-home/safeguarding/dsl-review -> 200 and GET /child-core-b-home/safeguarding/chronology/1098b5fe-83fa-4dac-a50d-f16f25bb37b9 -> 200 returned placeholder pages only.

assertions.issues: Blocked by the non-functional concern submission surface and missing DSL review/chronology implementations.

frictions.description: The invited staff user could not accept the invitation in one continuous flow: after registration the app redirected the new user to /onboarding, so I had to verify the email, log in, and then reopen the invitation URL to join the organisation.
frictions.resolution: Completed the invite by reusing the logged invitation URL after the staff account was verified and signed in.

blockers.description: The safeguarding concern form never emitted a POST request or navigated away after valid submit attempts, preventing creation of the prerequisite concern record.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried with direct click, semantic button click, a filled datetime-local value, and with the body-map toggle both on and off; every attempt stayed on the form with no console error and no POST request.

blockers.description: The DSL review and chronology routes are placeholder-only surfaces, and the Section 47/LADO routes are also placeholders rather than working tracking flows.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Opened each route directly as the appropriate user role and confirmed the placeholder text rendered instead of real workflow UI.

summary: Tested 7 assertions: 1 passed, 3 failed, and 3 were blocked by missing or non-functional safeguarding surfaces. Admissions workflow mostly works end-to-end, but declined referrals are not archived; safeguarding concern submission, DSL review, chronology, and restricted LADO/Section 47 tracking are incomplete.
